message:
perf(web): migrate all BTreeMap usages to HashMap and add router method dispatch fast-path

The web framework was using BTreeMap throughout for headers, path params,
query params, extensions, and router handler maps. None of these require
sorted iteration — they are keyed lookups — so BTreeMap's O(log n) per
access was paying for an ordering guarantee that was never consumed.

Request type (extract.rs):
- `headers: BTreeMap<String, String>` → HashMap
- `path_params: BTreeMap<String, String>` → HashMap
- `with_path_params()` parameter type updated to match

Response type (response.rs):
- `headers: BTreeMap<String, String>` → HashMap

Extensions (extract.rs):
- `data: BTreeMap<String, String>` → HashMap (comment updated to match)

Extractors (extract.rs):
- `Path<BTreeMap<String, String>>` → `Path<HashMap<String, String>>`
- `Query<BTreeMap<String, String>>` → `Query<HashMap<String, String>>`
- `Form<BTreeMap<String, String>>` → `Form<HashMap<String, String>>`
- `BTreeMap<String, String>` header map extractor → HashMap
- `parse_urlencoded()` return type → HashMap

Router (router.rs):
- `MethodRouter::handlers: BTreeMap<String, Box<dyn Handler>>` → HashMap
- `RoutePattern::matches()` return type → HashMap
- `dispatch()` gains a fast-path that checks `handlers.get(&req.method)`
  directly before falling back to `req.method.to_uppercase()`. Since
  virtually all real HTTP traffic already has uppercase methods, this
  avoids a String allocation on every dispatch.

Lab HTTP client (lab/http/client.rs):
- `RequestBuilder::headers: BTreeMap<String, String>` → HashMap

Test files updated throughout (handler.rs, request_region.rs, e2e_web.rs,
http_e2e.rs, http_verification.rs) to use HashMap for Path, Query, and
Form extractors, matching the new type signatures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
